contentBlocks.original:
सर्वेशं परमेशं श्रीपार्वतीशं वन्देऽहं विश्वेशं श्रीपन्नगेशम् ।
श्रीसाम्बं शम्भुं शिवं त्रैलोक्यपूज्यं वन्देऽहं त्रैनेत्रं श्रीकण्ठमीशम् ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvēśaṃ paramēśaṃ śrīpārvatīśaṃ vandē'haṃ viśvēśaṃ śrīpannagēśam ।
śrīsāmbaṃ śambhuṃ śivaṃ trailōkyapūjyaṃ vandē'haṃ trainētraṃ śrīkaṇṭhamīśam ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the lord of all — the supreme lord — the lord of Śrī Pārvatī — the lord of the universe — the lord of the sacred serpents — the auspicious Sāmba-Śambhu-Śiva — the lord of the three worlds — the one with the trident — the great Naṭarāja. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: sarveśam: lord of all | parameśam: supreme lord | śrī pārvatī-īśam: lord of Śrī Pārvatī | viśveśam: lord of the universe | śrī pannaga-īśam: lord of the sacred serpents | sāmbam: the one with Ambā | śambhum: the auspicious one | śivam: the auspicious/Śiva | trailokya-nātham: lord of the three worlds | śūlam dhāriṇam: bearer of the trident | mahā-naṭarājam: the great lord of dance

contentBlocks.original:
भस्माम्बरधरमीशं सुरपारिजातं बिल्वार्चितपदयुगलं सोमं सोमेशम् ।
जगदालयपरिशोभितदेवं परमात्मं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhasmāmbaradharamīśaṃ surapārijātaṃ bilvārchitapadayugalaṃ sōmaṃ sōmēśam ।
jagadālayapariśōbhitadēvaṃ paramātmaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the lord robed in sacred ash — the divine pārijāta tree for the gods — whose feet are worshipped with bilva leaves — Soma — the lord of the Moon — dwelling in the world as the divine abode — the supreme Ātman. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: bhasma-ambara-dhara: robed in sacred ash | sura-pārijāta: divine pārijāta tree for the gods | bilva-arcita-pāda-yugalam: feet worshipped with bilva leaves | somam: the moon-bearing Soma | soma-īśam: lord of the Moon | jagad-ālaya: dwelling/abode of the world | pariśobhita-devam: the divinely radiant deity | paramātmam: the supreme Ātman

contentBlocks.original:
कैलासप्रियवासं करुणाकरमीशं कात्यायनीविलसितप्रियवामभागम् ।
प्रणवार्चितमात्मार्चितं संसेवितरूपं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kailāsapriyavāsaṃ karuṇākaramīśaṃ kātyāyanīvilasitapriyavāmabhāgam ।
praṇavārchitamātmārchitaṃ saṃsēvitarūpaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the one who loves to dwell on Kailāsa — the compassionate lord — at whose beloved left side sports Kātyāyanī [Pārvatī] — who is the embodiment of the Praṇava (Oṃ) — who is worshipped by the self — whose form is served with devotion. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: kailāsa-priya-vāsam: who loves to dwell on Kailāsa | karuṇākara-īśam: the compassionate lord | kātyāyanī: Pārvatī (daughter of Katyāyana or Himavān, also an epithet of the Goddess) | vilasita: sporting/shining | priya-vāma-bhāgam: at the beloved left side | praṇava-arcitam: embodiment of the Praṇava (Oṃ) | ātma-arcitam: worshipped by the Self | saṃsevita-rūpam: whose form is served with devotion

contentBlocks.original:
मन्मथनिजमददहनं दाक्षायनीशं निर्गुणगुणसम्भरितं कैवल्यपुरुषम् ।
भक्तानुग्रहविग्रहमानन्दजैकं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manmathanijamadadahanaṃ dākṣāyanīśaṃ nirguṇaguṇasambharitaṃ kaivalyapuruṣam ।
bhaktānugrahavigrahamānandajaikaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the one who burned Manmatha's (Kāma's) pride of his own mada (intoxication with self-power) — the lord of Dākṣāyaṇī (Satī) — filled with the attributeless [Absolute] and overflowing with qualities — the Kaivalya Puruṣa — who is the very form of the grace bestowed on devotees — the singular source of bliss. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: manmatha-nija-mada-dahana: burning Manmatha's (Kāma's) pride and intoxication | dākṣāyaṇī-īśam: lord of Dākṣāyaṇī (Satī, daughter of Dakṣa) | nirguṇa-guṇa-sambhāritam: filled with the attributeless [Absolute] and overflowing with qualities | kaivalya-puruṣam: the Kaivalya Puruṣa (the person of absolute liberation/aloneness) | bhakta-anugraha-vigraham: whose form is the grace bestowed on devotees | ānanda-jaika: singular source of bliss

contentBlocks.original:
सुरगङ्गासम्प्लावितपावननिजशिखरं समभूषितशशिबिम्बं जटाधरं देवम् ।
निरतोज्ज्वलदावानलनयनफालभागं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suragaṅgāsamplāvitapāvananijaśikharaṃ samabhūṣitaśaśibimbaṃ jaṭādharaṃ dēvam ।
niratōjjvaladāvānalanayanaphālabhāgaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the one whose own peak is flooded by the divine Gaṅgā — the god bearing the equally adorned crescent moon — the jaṭādhara (wearing matted locks) — at whose forehead-portion the fire of the eyes (third eye) blazes continuously, bright and unwavering. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: sura-gaṅgā-samplāvita-pāvana-nija-śikharaṃ: whose own peak is flooded and purified by the divine Gaṅgā | sama-bhūṣita-śaśi-bimba: equally adorned with the moon's orb | jaṭādhara: wearing matted locks | nirata-ujjvala-dāvānala-nayana-phāla-bhāgam: at whose forehead-portion the fire of the blazing eyes burns continuously

contentBlocks.original:
शशिसूर्यनेत्रद्वयमाराध्यपुरुषं सुरकिन्नरपन्नगमयमीशं सङ्काशम् ।
शरवणभवसम्पूजितनिजपादपद्मं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaśisūryanētradvayamārādhyapuruṣaṃ surakinnarapannagamayamīśaṃ saṅkāśam ।
śaravaṇabhavasampūjitanijapādapadmaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Āradhya Puruṣa (the person worthy of worship) whose two eyes are the Moon and Sun — who is manifest as the assembled gods, Kinnaras, and serpents — whose own lotus-feet are worshipped by the one born in Śaravaṇa (Skanda). [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: śaśi-sūrya-netra-dvayam: whose two eyes are the Moon and Sun | āradhya-puruṣam: the person worthy of worship | sura-kinnara-pannaga-maya-īśam-saṅkāśam: who is manifest as the assembled gods, Kinnaras, and serpents | śaravaṇa-bhava-sampūjita-nija-pāda-padmaṃ: whose own lotus-feet are worshipped by the one born in Śaravaṇa (Skanda/Kārttikeya)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशैलपुरवासं ईशं मल्लीशं श्रीकालहस्तीशं स्वर्णमुखीवासम् ।
काञ्चीपुरमीशं श्रीकामाक्षीतेजं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśailapuravāsaṃ īśaṃ mallīśaṃ śrīkālahastīśaṃ svarṇamukhīvāsam ।
kāñchīpuramīśaṃ śrīkāmākṣītējaṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the one who dwells at Śrīśaila — the lord Mallīśa — and at Śrī Kāḷahastī — who dwells at Svarṇamukhī — the lord at Kāñcīpura — the brilliance of Śrī Kāmākṣī — the auspicious great Mahādeva. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: śrīśaila-pura-vāsam: dwelling at Śrīśaila | īśam mallīśam: the lord Mallīśa (Mallikārjuna at Śrīśaila) | śrī-kāḷahastī-īśam: lord at Śrī Kāḷahastī | svarṇamukhī-vāsam: dwelling at Svarṇamukhī | kāñcīpura-īśam: lord at Kāñcīpura | śrī kāmākṣī-tejam: brilliance of Śrī Kāmākṣī | mahādeva: the great god

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिपुरान्तकमीशं अरुणाचलेशं दक्षिणामूर्तिं गुरुं लोकपूज्यम् ।
चिदम्बरपुरवासं पञ्चलिङ्गमूर्तिं वन्देऽहं शिवशङ्करमीशं देवेशम् ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tripurāntakamīśaṃ aruṇāchalēśaṃ dakṣiṇāmūrtiṃ guruṃ lōkapūjyam ।
chidambarapuravāsaṃ pañchaliṅgamūrtiṃ vandē'haṃ śivaśaṅkaramīśaṃ dēvēśam ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the destroyer of Tripura — the lord of Aruṇācala — Dakṣiṇāmūrti — the Guru revered throughout the world — dwelling at Cidambara — the five-liṅga-mūrti — the twelve-Jyotirlinga form — the great Mahādeva. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: tripurāntaka: destroyer of Tripura | aruṇācala-īśam: lord of Aruṇācala | dakṣiṇāmūrtim: the south-facing form (teacher under the banyan tree) | gurum loka-pūjyam: Guru revered by all the world | cidambara-pura-vāsam: dwelling at Cidambara | pañca-liṅga-mūrtiṃ: the five-liṅga form | dvādaśa-jyotirlinga-rūpam: the twelve Jyotirlinga form

contentBlocks.original:
ज्योतिर्मयशुभलिङ्गं सङ्ख्यात्रयनाट्यं त्रयीवेद्यमाद्यं पञ्चाननमीशम् ।
वेदाद्भुतगात्रं वेदार्णवजनितं वेदाग्रं विश्वाग्रं श्रीविश्वनाथम् ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyōtirmayaśubhaliṅgaṃ saṅkhyātrayanāṭyaṃ trayīvēdyamādyaṃ pañchānanamīśam ।
vēdādbhutagātraṃ vēdārṇavajanitaṃ vēdāgraṃ viśvāgraṃ śrīviśvanātham ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the auspicious light-form liṅga — who performs the three-count dance — who is known through the three Vedas — the primordial one — the five-faced lord — whose form is wonderful as the Vedas — who is the meaning/essence of the Vedas — the lord of all the worlds — the great Mahādeva. [Āratī to Śiva]
contentBlocks.meanings: jyotirmaya-śubha-liṅgam: the auspicious light-form liṅga | saṃkhyā-traya-nāṭyam: performing the three-count dance | trayī-vedyam: known through the three Vedas | ādyam: the primordial | pañcānana: five-faced | veda-adbhuta-gātram: wonderful form as the Vedas | veda-artham: the essence/meaning of the Vedas | loka-patim: lord of all the worlds | mahādeva: the great god
